name: Pairwise Sorted Comparison
summary: For each unplaced word, scan the rest and group any word whose sorted form matches.
visualizationIntent: init: the word array strs is shown as cells, no pointers, groups empty; loop(i): pointer i marks the anchor word, already-grouped cells dim to visited; check(i used): a dimmed anchor is skipped; update(new group): anchor flips current and starts current_group aux; loop(j)+check: pointer j scans rightward comparing sorted_i vs sorted_j via the readout chip; update(match): on is_anagram the j-cell flips result and its word glides into the current_group aux, then dims to visited; return: every cell visited, GROUPS holds the final partition.

lineExplanations.3: Start with an empty list that will hold each finished anagram group.
lineExplanations.4: Track, per word, whether it has already been placed into some group.
lineExplanations.5: Walk through every word by index i â€” each is a candidate anchor for a new group.
lineExplanations.6: If this word was already pulled into an earlier group, it can't start a new one.
lineExplanations.7: Skip it and move on to the next anchor.
lineExplanations.8: Open a new group seeded with the anchor word itself.
lineExplanations.9: Mark the anchor as used so it is never grouped again.
lineExplanations.10: Scan every later word j to see whether it belongs in this group.
lineExplanations.11: If word j is already grouped, there is nothing to compare.
lineExplanations.12: Skip it and try the next candidate.
lineExplanations.13: Sort the anchor word's letters â€” anagrams share the same sorted form.
lineExplanations.14: Sort candidate word j's letters the same way.
lineExplanations.15: The two words are anagrams exactly when their sorted forms are identical.
lineExplanations.16: When they match, word j joins the current group.
lineExplanations.17: Add word j to the group being built.
lineExplanations.18: Mark word j as used so no later anchor grabs it.
lineExplanations.19: The group is complete â€” store it in the answer.
lineExplanations.20: Return the list of all groups.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the wrapper class the platform runs.
syntaxExplanations.2: def groupAnagrams(self, strs): the method receiving the list of words.
syntaxExplanations.4: [False] * len(strs) builds a list of False flags, one per word.
syntaxExplanations.5: for i in range(len(strs)) loops i over every index 0..n-1.
syntaxExplanations.6: if used[i]: reads the boolean flag at index i.
syntaxExplanations.7: continue jumps straight to the next loop iteration.
syntaxExplanations.8: [strs[i]] is a one-element list holding the anchor word.
syntaxExplanations.9: used[i] = True stores True at index i.
syntaxExplanations.10: range(i + 1, len(strs)) yields the indices after i.
syntaxExplanations.11: if used[j]: reads the flag at index j.
syntaxExplanations.12: continue skips to the next j.
syntaxExplanations.13: sorted(strs[i]) returns the word's characters as a sorted list.
syntaxExplanations.14: sorted(strs[j]) does the same for word j.
syntaxExplanations.15: == compares the two sorted lists for equality.
syntaxExplanations.16: if is_anagram: branches on the boolean we just computed.
syntaxExplanations.17: current_group.append(strs[j]) adds the word to the group.
syntaxExplanations.18: used[j] = True marks word j as placed.
syntaxExplanations.20: return groups hands back the final answer.
